source.title: Hypothalamic volume is associated with body mass index in patients with Prader-Labhart-Willi syndrome.

confidence_rationale: Structural neuroimaging in Prader-Willi syndrome (the canonical clinical model of pathological insatiable hunger) shows hypothalamic volume changes correlating with BMI. PWS is the human existence proof for what happens when the hunger-override system runs without a regulatory shutoff: the patient eats relentlessly despite obesity and metabolic harm. For V3, this is a clinical phenotype where the override authority's regulatory layer is missing, producing a sustained drive_level that cannot be arbitrated down. Confidence moderate-high because the clinical phenotype is well-characterised but the imaging-genotype-behaviour mechanistic chain is correlational.
failure_signatures: Insatiable hunger despite obesity -- when override authority lacks regulatory shutoff, drive_level remains saturated regardless of metabolic state.
Hypothalamic structural alteration correlates with BMI -- the substrate of the override regulator is altered in a graded way, supporting graded rather than all-or-nothing regulator function.

mapping.source_claim_statement: MRI volumetric analysis in adults with Prader-Willi syndrome shows hypothalamic volume reductions correlating with BMI. PWS is genetic (paternal 15q11-q13 imprinting loss) and produces lifelong hyperphagia with severe obesity. Hypothalamic structural alteration is consistent with the clinical hypothesis of impaired satiety / impaired drive-regulation.
mapping.ree_translation: PWS is the human pathology equivalent of an SD-036/SD-012 regulator-failure mode. The clinical phenotype tells V3 what failure looks like: drive_level saturated (insatiable hunger), no shutdown signal arrives despite metabolic surplus (obesity). For V3 architecture, this argues that the regulator layer is REQUIRED for safety -- absence of regulator does not produce zero drive (which would be safe) but produces saturated drive (which is pathological). This has implications for the SD-036 fix to V3-EXQ-471: the regulator must be bidirectional (shutdown-after-need-met as well as override-when-need-overrides-fear), not just one-directional.
mapping.mapping_caveat: PWS is a complex multi-system genetic syndrome, not a clean knockout of a single regulator. The hypothalamic volume changes could be cause, consequence, or correlate of the hyperphagia. The mechanistic link between volume change and behavioural phenotype is correlational.
mapping.source_context: MRI volumetric analysis in adults with genetically-confirmed PWS, comparing to controls.

confidence_components.notes: Brain Communications, well-characterised clinical sample. Mapping fidelity moderate -- the override-failure interpretation is supported by clinical phenotype but the mechanism is correlational.
